feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: OB-GYN group makes vaccine recommendations for the first time
entry.summary: A prominent OB-GYN group announced a set of vaccine recommendations on Wednesday that differs from what the U.S. government advises. The immunization schedule is specifically for pregnant, postpartum and breastfeeding women. It aligns with prior recommendatio…

entry.title: GLP-1 Users Get Surgery for 'Ozempic Ears' Side Effect
entry.summary: And you thought “Ozempic butt” was the worst of it.

entry.title: Stanford lays out plans for the biggest medical facility in its history
entry.summary: Stanford Medicine plans to begin construction of a major new cancer center in Redwood City as soon as 2029 that will serve the growing number of patients.

entry.title: I'm a Stanford longevity researcher: My 85-year-old Italian mother's 5 rules for a long, happy life are so simple
entry.summary: Dr. Lucia Aronica has studied aging for over 20 years. She shares the happiness and longevity lessons passed down by her 85-year-old mother.

entry.title: 1 in 3 adults who don’t know how to dose medications. Are you one of them?
entry.summary: Medical visits are often quick, leaving patients to rely on written instructions. But many aren’t taught to interpret the information they are given, a study says.

entry.title: Rabid foxes found near popular trail in Baltimore’s Roland Park
entry.summary: Baltimore City officials are warning residents about rabid foxes found in Roland Park.

entry.title: 3 ways GLP-1 drugs could be getting better
entry.summary: Drug companies are racing to catch up to Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk, seeking to require fewer shots or reduce side effects.

entry.title: Older drug reprograms white blood cells, improves blood sugar in type 1 diabetes
entry.summary: (This is an excerpt of the Health Rounds newsletter, where we present latest medical studies on Tuesdays and Thursdays.) June 10 (Reuters) - We also report...

entry.title: Dangerous tick-borne disease discovered again in Calif.
entry.summary: A fourth person has become sickened by a rare bacteria first discovered in Sonoma County.

entry.title: This Overlooked Health Marker Is Linked To Better Longevity — And You've Probably Never Heard Of It
entry.summary: Increasing this can boost your brain, decrease anxiety, improve cardiovascular health and more.

entry.title: Is the peptide craze backed by science? The promise behind the hype
entry.summary: Peptide injections are the hottest trend in wellness. Researchers say enthusiasm for these unregulated drugs has got ahead of the science.

entry.title: The infectious diseases that experts worry could spread during the World Cup
entry.summary: Officials are less worried about viruses in the headlines, and more concerned about illnesses like measles and dengue, as well as covid-19 and sexually transmitted infections.

entry.title: Trump’s war on state capacity is coming home to roost
entry.summary: From the screwworm to Ebola and beyond, there��s a price to pay.

entry.title: How to talk to someone who has dementia
entry.summary: Dementia makes communication more challenging, but these practices and tips can help you stay connected through the changes

entry.title: My Stepsister Went Off the Rails When Our Dad Died. Now I’m Not Sure What I Owe Her.
entry.summary: I'm so confused.

entry.title: 6 Herbs That May Help You Sleep Better Than Melatonin
entry.summary: Discover which herbs can help you sleep better, such as valerian, chamomile, lavender, passionflower, and ashwagandha, and how they promote more restful sleep.

entry.title: Mama Golden Retriever Rescued From Life in a Cage Thinks Little Kids Are Her Babies
entry.summary: It’s a special dog that gets to spend her Gotcha Day surrounded by toys, family, and a fan club of tiny humans who she seems convinced belong to her. Over on...

entry.title: Popular joint supplement glucosamine linked to faster Alzheimer’s progression
entry.summary: A major study suggests glucosamine, a popular supplement for joint pain, could be linked to faster progression from mild cognitive impairment to Alzheimer’s disease. Researchers found a 25% higher likelihood of developing dementia among glucosamine users and …

entry.title: “A Lifetime Of Consequences”: 61 Cool And Interesting Medical Facts
entry.summary: At this point, it can feel like we already know almost everything there is to know about the human body. After all, doctors and scientists have spent centuries studying it. But the truth is, new discoveries are being made all the time, and there’s always some…

entry.title: World's First AI‑Designed Vaccine Tested in Humans For The First Time
entry.summary: Researchers at the University of Cambridge have developed what they describe as a fundamentally new type of vaccine using artificial intelligence (AI).

entry.title: “It’s Silly But It Works”: People Are Revealing The Tiny Habits That Help Them Walk More Without Even Realizing It
entry.summary: Writing all of these down immediately.

entry.title: Is a Hidden Vitamin D Deficiency the Reason You Can’t Lose Weight? See the Symptoms and Easy Fixes
entry.summary: Could a sneaky vitamin D deficiency be the reason you can't lose weight or always feel tired? Learn the symptoms and how to boost your levels naturally.

entry.title: If you feel that the world is becoming less intelligent in some way, the data from Norway, Denmark, Finland, France, Britain, and Australia agrees with you, with average IQ scores in those countries falling continuously since the mid-1990s by an estimated five to
entry.summary: The finding is one of the most carefully documented and least understood in contemporary cognitive science. For most of the twentieth century, average IQ scores in industrialised countries rose at a consistent pace. The increase, documented across multiple te…

entry.title: 'I felt lighter, more open': Why you are never too old for therapy
entry.summary: We assume therapy belongs to youth, yet older people may be particularly likely to benefit from psychological support.

entry.title: 2026 AHA/ACC/ADA/ASN Guideline for the Prevention, Detection, Evaluation, and Management of Cardiovascular-Kidney-Metabolic Syndrome
entry.summary: Guideline offering updated evidence on cardiovascular‑kidney‑metabolic syndrome for clinicians managing obesity, diabetes, CKD, and cardiovascular disease.

entry.title: Advanced radiotherapy for prostate cancer to cut sessions from 20 to five
entry.summary: Some men in England with the disease will now be offered an advanced form of treatment on the NHS.

entry.title: The Best Time to Eat Dinner If You’re Trying to Lower Your Cholesterol
entry.summary: When you eat your dinner may impact your cholesterol numbers. Dietitians explain when to eat and what to put on your plate for better heart health.

entry.title: Everett Gospel Mission plans expansion to address growing homelessness crisis
entry.summary: With demand increasing for shelter services at a facility that is already operating at capacity, the Everett Gospel Mission is moving forward with a $30 million

entry.title: Colon Cancer May Have More to Do with Gut Health Than We Realize, New Study Says
entry.summary: A new study links gut health changes after precancerous polyp removal to higher colon cancer risk, suggesting diet and lifestyle matter for long-term health.

entry.title: Beyond the tremor: Unveiling the invisible struggle of Parkinson's Disease
entry.summary: Parkinson's disease, isn't always marked by a visible tremor. The true experience often lies hidden beneath the surface – an invisible struggle impacting millions.

entry.title: Glucosamine supplements may speed memory loss from Alzheimer’s, new research shows
entry.summary: Animal experiments and analysis of patient records suggest that taking glucosamine is safe for a healthy brain but is associated with further decline in diseased brains.

entry.title: By 2030, Korean women are projected to become the first population in human history with an average life expectancy above 90 years — exceeding even Japan — according to a Lancet study of 35 industrialized nations, in a demographic shift driven by impro
entry.summary: In February 2017, an international team led by Majid Ezzati, professor of global environmental health at Imperial College London, published a paper in The Lancet projecting future life expectancy across 35 industrialised countries. The study, conducted in col…

entry.title: New study recommends 1 alcoholic drink per day. Its researchers want clearer U.S. guidelines on drinking
entry.summary: One of the officials involved in the study commissioned by Biden's Democratic administration accused Trump's Republican administration of “sidelining” the research — an allegation the Trump administration denies.

entry.title: Take charge of your health: men’s preventive services covered by TRICARE
entry.summary: When’s the last time you went to the doctor for a check-up? Now’s a great time to check in on your health and learn about the men’s health and preventive services that TRICARE covers. TRICARE covers a

entry.title: There’s a Terrible Reason Why This Ebola Outbreak Is Different
entry.summary: It wouldn’t have been this way a few years ago.

entry.title: New research links even low alcohol consumption to cancer, heart disease, and premature death
entry.summary: Even what many Americans consider moderate drinking is linked to an increased risk of death, disability, and chronic diseases such as cancer and heart disease, according to a new study published.  The findings show mortality risk from alcohol of 1 i…

entry.title: A Commonly Used Sleep Aid May Have a Dangerous Side Effect
entry.summary: Around 1 in 5 US adults are thought to struggle with sleep health, and for many of us, the search for rest eventually leads to medication.

entry.title: Boy's rash 'slipped my mind', doctor tells inquest
entry.summary: A doctor forgot to look for a reported rash on a boy who died from a strep A infection, a court hears.

entry.title: New Study Exposes Problem With Protein Products As Experts Warn Labels May Mislead Consumers About Benefits
entry.summary: As protein products flood store shelves, researchers are questioning whether their healthy reputation is backed by evidence.

entry.title: Big tobacco hooked us on ultra-processed foods. It might teach us how to cut back
entry.summary: Research published in the American Journal of Public Health details the connection between ultra-processed foods and the tobacco industry when it comes to production, strategy and marketing.

entry.title: 5 Wall Exercises That Strengthen Inner Thighs Faster Than Gym Machines After 60
entry.summary: A Pilates instructor shares 5 wall exercises that strengthen your inner thighs and support balance after 60. No machines needed.

entry.title: The Kombucha Surprise: One Ingredient Dramatically Changes Its Chemistry and Health Potential
entry.summary: Tea choice significantly changes kombucha’s chemistry, flavor, and antioxidant potential, with green and oolong teas showing the strongest biological activity.

entry.title: WSU study finds father’s health before conception may affect future children
entry.summary: A father's health before conception may play a role in the future health of his children, according to new research from Washington State University.The study h

entry.title: World Needs to Find the 'Will' to Contain Ebola
entry.summary: Ex-USAID official says it will be 'enormous' task to stem what may be the worst outbreak ever

entry.title: Three more US passengers from hantavirus-hit ship return to home states
entry.summary: June 9 () - Three passengers from the hantavirus-affected MV Hondius cruise ship have returned to their home states ‌in the U. after four weeks of monitoring...

entry.title: How your attachment style is linked to the way you experience being alone
entry.summary: Do you enjoy your alone time, or do you feel forced into it? A recent study found that people with anxious and avoidant attachment styles frequently experience "non-self-determined solitude," a state strongly associated with intense loneliness.

entry.title: Research Shows There Is a Specific Age When Your Strength and Fitness Start To Decline
entry.summary: Fitness decline may start around age 35. Learn what the study found and how staying active can help slow age-related losses.

entry.title: World-first: therapy to make cells young again given to a person
entry.summary: The first participant has been treated in a landmark clinical trial of cellular reprogramming, which aims to rejuvenate ageing cells.

entry.title: Hyperglycosylation is a metabolic driver of Alzheimer’s disease
entry.summary: Through spatial multiomics, spatial stable isotope tracing and retrospective cohort analyses, this study identifies hyperglycosylation as a driver of Alzheimer’s disease and a promising therapeutic target.

entry.title: A Dietitian Shares What She Eats When Her Brain Feels Foggy
entry.summary: A dietitian shares what she eats in a day to support brain health, improve focus and reduce brain fog, along with other practical tips.
